content: The EN-OS sovereign agentic infrastructure now features an actuarial-grade 'Single Pane of Glass' observability dashboard. This dashboard, built with Streamlit, provides zero-friction local filesystem querying across four primary operational quadrants, offering comprehensive insight into system health and performance.
one_liner: A 'Single Pane of Glass' Streamlit dashboard provides actuarial-grade observability for EN-OS agentic infrastructure.
source_context: EPIC #106 Implementation Plan: EN-OS Instrumentation & Universal Dashboard - Goal Description

content: A core architectural decision for the EN-OS dashboard was to utilize native Streamlit components for charting and display. This approach was specifically chosen to maintain a lightweight dependency footprint, minimizing overhead and simplifying deployment for the observability solution.
one_liner: Native Streamlit components were prioritized for charting to ensure a lightweight dependency footprint for the EN-OS dashboard.
source_context: Architectural Strategy & Libraries - Charting: Native Streamlit components

content: Agents must append an `[ACKNOWLEDGED] All halts cleared by Admin` timestamp to the `failsafes.log` when clearing a halt, rather than deleting the file. This ensures the preservation of a complete forensic trail and audit history for all safety state transitions.
one_liner: Always append an `[ACKNOWLEDGED]` flag to `failsafes.log` to clear halts, preserving the forensic audit trail.
source_context: User Review Required - Safety State Mnemonic

content: We've successfully completed Phase 1 of our EN-OS development, focusing on Security & Observability! This milestone includes the creation of our universal dashboard and the crucial securing of token circuits, significantly enhancing our agentic infrastructure's resilience and transparency.
one_liner: Phase 1: Security & Observability for EN-OS is complete, featuring a universal dashboard and secured token circuits.
source_context: EN-OS Dashboard (Epic #106) Implementation Walkthrough - Summary of Changes

content: A key safety feature, 'Resurrection Bay,' has been developed within the EN-OS dashboard. This allows administrators to precisely respawn native agents from an exact issue marker in the background, providing robust recovery capabilities for halted workflows while maintaining an annotated audit history.
one_liner: Introducing 'Resurrection Bay': precise native agent respawns from issue markers for robust workflow recovery.

content: Successful implementation of the EN-OS dashboard required robust interoperability between Python and native system commands. Test runs confirmed that standard PowerShell commands like `pm2 jlist` and `Get-Service` execute effectively within Python wrappers, enabling real-time monitoring of daemon connectivity.
one_liner: Python wrappers successfully execute native PowerShell commands for real-time daemon monitoring in EN-OS.
source_context: Walkthrough - Verification: Test runs confirm that standard PowerShell `pm2 jlist` and Active services execute effectively inside Python wrappers.

content: The instruction to launch the EN-OS dashboard is `streamlit run scripts\enos_dashboard.py` from the `global_agent` directory. While useful for operation, this is a routine command and not a significant decision, problem solved, or architectural insight.
one_liner: Simple command to launch the Streamlit dashboard.
source_context: Walkthrough - To launch the pane of glass, simply run: `streamlit run scripts\enos_dashboard.py`
